text: Step 1: connection-weight math helper + backend explanation persistence
description: DONE 2026-05-31. Added packages/shared/src/connection-weight.ts (weightToShare, shareToWeight, defaultWeight, clampShare, MAX_SHARE=0.9, isSoleContributor) + 11 passing unit tests; exported from shared index. Threaded optional `explanation` through backend connectAimsInternal (server.ts) and connectAims + createSubAim/createFloatingAim/createAimInPhase (aim.ts); preserves explanation across connection reorder. shared+backend tsc clean.

reflections.context: Build the foundation for the contribution-% connection modal: a pure share<->weight math module and backend persistence of connection explanations.
reflections.outcome: Added shared/src/connection-weight.ts with 11 passing tests and threaded an optional `explanation` through connectAimsInternal and all create/connect mutations. shared+backend type-check clean.
reflections.effectiveness: Putting the math in shared (not the component) means frontend live-preview and backend/MCP can reuse one source of truth. Discovered the value engine normalizes by ΣsiblingWeights + loopWeight, which corrected the percentage formula (loop must be in the denominator).
reflections.lesson: Verify the actual normalization in value-calculation.ts before defining percentage<->weight math; the loopWeight term is easy to miss and changes the 100%/default rules. Also: backend `npm test` is broken independent of changes — test files import vitest but run under node --test ('Vitest failed to find the runner'); rely on tsx --test for individual suites + tsc for verification.
reflections.pattern: Same as economic-model work: ground UI/derived math in the existing propagation engine rather than re-deriving it.

status.comment: Math helper + tests + backend persistence landed; type-checks clean.
